title: Young scientists showcase new ideas
description: MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Mossel Bay learners are...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Mossel Bay learners are among the top achievers of the Eskom Expo Eden Karoo Regional Science Fair, where young scientists showcased their innovative research projects.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Mossel Bay learners are among the top achievers of the Eskom Expo Eden Karoo Regional Science Fair, where young scientists showcased their innovative research projects."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The event took place last weekend at the George City Hall."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grade 10s, Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos of Point High won a Special Eskom Award for the Best Female project, while Grade 11s Joël Oosthuizen and Jonathan van Wyk, also of Point High, scooped the Best Energy Project award.","html":"Grade 10s, Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos of Point High won a Special Eskom Award for the Best Female project, while Grade 11s Joël Oosthuizen and Jonathan van Wyk, also of Point High, scooped the Best Energy Project award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grade 11 learner Heinrich Conradie of Hoërskool Outeniqua in George impressed the adjudicators with his scale model of a robotic vehicle, complete with a camera, humidity, temperature and quality sensors.","html":"Grade 11 learner Heinrich Conradie of Hoërskool Outeniqua in George impressed the adjudicators with his scale model of a robotic vehicle, complete with a camera, humidity, temperature and quality sensors."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This invention can be used in a CBRN (chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear) contaminated area to gather information. He secured the Eskom prize for Best Innovation with this remote-controled vehicle."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Other winners of the sought-after Eskom prizes include and Grade 9 learner Linathi Koyana of Murray High in Plettenberg Bay (Best Development Project).","html":"Other winners of the sought-after Eskom prizes include and Grade 9 learner Linathi Koyana of Murray High in Plettenberg Bay (Best Development Project)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"George Herald attended the event on Saturday 16 August, observing the interaction between the learners at the fair.","html":"George Herald attended the event on Saturday 16 August, observing the interaction between the learners at the fair."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It was an honour to witness these top achievers excitedly sharing their inventions, giving a breakdown of their work to interested visitors and other participants.","html":"It was an honour to witness these top achievers excitedly sharing their inventions, giving a breakdown of their work to interested visitors and other participants."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Izelke and Sarai designed a three-tier, handbag size water filter which operates with activated charcoal made of black wattle wood, an invasive species. This was developed for people in rural areas, providing a cost effective way to purify water.","html":"Izelke and Sarai designed a three-tier, handbag size water filter which operates with activated charcoal made of black wattle wood, an invasive species. This was developed for people in rural areas, providing a cost effective way to purify water."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Joël and Jonathan designed a system that uses wind energy as fuel source to generate electricity and can charge an electric vehicle while driving."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grade 7 learners Jakequinn Saayman and Mikash Maritz of Kretzenshoop Primary in George won a prize for the Best Junior Engineering project, and Grade 7s Mqweba Khalima and Mnyanda Iminathi of Delville Park Primary in George won a prize for the Best Junior Social Sciences project.","html":"Grade 7 learners Jakequinn Saayman and Mikash Maritz of Kretzenshoop Primary in George won a prize for the Best Junior Engineering project, and Grade 7s Mqweba Khalima and Mnyanda Iminathi of Delville Park Primary in George won a prize for the Best Junior Social Sciences project."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The fair featured 49 projects showcased by 74 young scientists across Grades 4 - 12 and Technical and Vocational Education and Training Level 2 to 4 students.","html":"The fair featured 49 projects showcased by 74 young scientists across Grades 4 - 12 and Technical and Vocational Education and Training Level 2 to 4 students."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Learners were recognised for excellence in research and innovation and 12 gold, five silver and 16 bronze medals were awarded.","html":"Learners were recognised for excellence in research and innovation and 12 gold, five silver and 16 bronze medals were awarded."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grade 10s Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos of Point High with their water purifying system. Photo: Michelle PienaarRecipients of the Special Awards from Eskom:","html":"Grade 10s Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos of Point High with their water purifying system.
Photo: Michelle Pienaar
Recipients of the Special Awards from Eskom:"},{"type":"list","items":["Best Female: Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos, Grade 10, Punt High","Best Innovation: Heinrich Conradie, Grade 11, Outeniqua (sponsored by Steyn-Wilson Laboratories, Zebra Surfacing and Martin & East Group): Agriculture (Best Junior): Aedan Rischmuller, Grade 9, Still Bay HomeschoolComputer science and software development (Best Senior): Somila Sihele, Grade 10, Murray High","Earth Sciences (Best Senior): Izelke Conradie and Sarai Roos, Grade 10, Punt High","Earth Sciences (Best Junior): Reneshmee Raymond and Ke-ano Jacobs, Grade 6, De Villiers Primary","Energy (Best Senior): Joel Oosthuizen and Jonathan van Wyk, Grade 11, Punt High","Engineering (Best Senior): Heinrich Conradie, Grade 11, Outeniqua High","Engineering (Best Junior): Jakequinn Saayman and Mikash Maritz, Grade 7, Kretzenshoop Primary","Environmental Sciences (Best Senior): Thandile Mqeke, Grade 11, Concordia High","Environmental Sciences (Best Junior): Ivile Ncanywa, Grade 9, Murray High","Social Sciences (Best Senior): Haily Ruth Petersen, Grade 11, Aurial College Social Sciences (Best Junior): Mqweba Khalima and Mnyanda Iminathi, Grade 7, Delville Park Primary"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Cash prizes by Rondebult:","html":"Cash prizes by Rondebult:"},{"type":"list","items":["Best Senior Male: Heinrich Conradie, Grade 11, Outeniqua High","Best Senior Female: Izelke Conradie & Sarai Roos, Grade 10, Punt High","Best Junior Male: Aedan Rischmuller, Grade 9, Still Bay Homeschool"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Additionally, the SA Quantum Technology Initiative at Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT) awarded a Raspberry Pi to James Julies, Grade 11, Morester Senior Secondary.","html":"Additionally, the SA Quantum Technology Initiative at Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT) awarded a Raspberry Pi to James Julies, Grade 11, Morester Senior Secondary."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
